master_row.title: Modal Frequency Relation
master_row.canonical_statement: x^\mu) e^{iny/R_\text{KK}} \end{equation} \subsection{4D Effective Theory} Each mode $\phi_n$ appears as massive field: \begin{equation} \left(\Box_{(4)} + \frac{n^2}{R_\text{KK}^2}\right)\phi_n = 0 \end{equation} \textbf{Critical Observation}: If $R_\text{KK} \sim 10^{-6}$ m, then: \begin{equation} f_n = \frac{nc}{2\pi R_\text{KK}} \sim n \times 50 \text{ GHz} \end{equation} This is in the \emph{acoustic-accessible regime} for ultrasonics! \section{Resonance Conditions} \subsection{Mode Coupling Criterion} \begin{theorem}[Harmonic-KK Resonance] Magneto-acoustic modes couple to KK tower when: \begin{equation} \omega_{nl}^\text{ac} = \omega_{n'l'}^B + \frac{mc}{R_\text{KK}} \quad (m \in \mathbb{Z}) \end{equation} \end{theorem} \subsection{Selection Rules} From angular momentum conservation: \begin{align} \Delta l &= 0, \pm 1, \pm 2 \\ \Delta m &= 0, \pm 1 \end{align} \section{Observable Signatures} \subsection{Predicted Spectrum} Expected absorption lines at: \begin{equation} f_\text{resonance} = f_0 \sqrt{1 + n^2\left(\frac{\lambda_\text{acoustic}}{\lambda_\text{KK}}\right)^2} \end{equation} \subsection{Topological Transition} At critical coupling $\lambda_g > \lambda_c$, the syste
